text: Because if you're linked, if you're not even good enough, if you're not even confident enough to make your LinkedIn sound badass, then no amount of publicly available information is going to help us spot that hidden gem, right?
context: Explaining that polish matters for identifying hidden talent and that raw LinkedIn data can’t surface the real gems.

text: Instead of a LinkedIn profile, you have a career span profile.
context: Framing CareerSpan as the signal that can plug into TeamWork the way a LinkedIn profile would otherwise.

text: We break every role and every individual down to their component skills and responsibilities, and then what we do is we effectively look at this and say, hey, how many of these are you missing in general? How many of these do you have, but you are maybe, you know, you haven't told a story about? And then anything you don't see is stuff that we've already covered.
context: Describing the matching engine’s gap analysis alignment between candidate stories and role demands.

text: It will customize everything specifically to that role and it will rewrite the skills, it will rewrite the actual bullet points, it turns everything into really high quality punchy stuff.
context: Selling the tailored resume technology that rewrites skills and bullets per role.
